name: Table
summary: Data table with sortable columns, smart height/auto-pagination, and unified `selectionMode` (`'none'|'single'|'multiple'`) keyed by `getItemKey` for selection that survives data refreshes (the `selectable`/`rowKey` props are deprecated aliases); use it for tabular data with columns.

props.name: onSelectionChange
props.description:
Fired only on user interaction (checkbox toggle) and when pruning drops
rows â€” the unified SelectionProps.onSelectionChange, emitting the
selected **rows** (Table's selection unit is `T`).

props.name: onRowClick
props.type: (row: T, rowIndex: number, event: React.MouseEvent<HTMLTableRowElement>) => void
props.description:
Fired when a row is clicked (ignores clicks that originate from obvious interactive children
like buttons, inputs, or links). Receives the row object, its visible index, and the click event.

props.name: minConstrainedRows
props.description:
Minimum body rows that must be visible for an internal scrollbar to be used.
If the constrained viewport would show fewer rows than this, height constraining is disabled
to avoid a tiny scroller. Default: 4.

props.name: maxExpandedRows
props.description:
Applies only when `minConstrainedRows` disables the usual constraint. When the dataset
exceeds this size, pagination kicks in (via <Pagination/>) and shows this many rows
per page instead of using an internal scrollbar. Default: 30.

props.name: paginate
props.description:
If true, always paginate using `maxExpandedRows` rows per page, regardless of
`minConstrainedRows`/`constrainHeight`. When false (default), pagination engages
automatically only when `minConstrainedRows` disables constraint and the dataset
exceeds `maxExpandedRows`.

props.name: page
props.description:
Controlled current page (1-based) when `paginate` is true or auto-pagination engages.
If omitted, the table manages its own page state.

props.name: onPageChange
props.description: Called when the page changes (receives the 1-based page number). Used with controlled `page`.

props.name: paginationWindow
props.description:
Controls the width of the visible window of page buttons when pagination renders.
Passed to <Pagination visibleWindow>. Optional; defaults to Paginationâ€™s internal behavior.

props.name: preset
props.description: One or many style-preset names registered via `definePreset()`

props.name: selectionMode
props.description:
How many items may be selected at once.
- `'none'` â€” selection is disabled (the default for components that gate it).
- `'single'` â€” at most one item is selected.
- `'multiple'` â€” any number of items may be selected.

Canonical replacement for the per-component flags (Table's `selectable`).

props.name: selected
props.description: Controlled selection as an array of selection units (`K`).

props.name: defaultSelected
props.description: Uncontrolled initial selection as an array of selection units (`K`).

props.name: getItemKey
props.type: keyof T | ((item: T, index: number) => string | number) | undefined
props.description:
Stable identity for each item â€” a property name or a function of the item
and its index. Canonical, cross-component replacement for Table's `rowKey`
and List's `getKey`. When omitted, components fall back to reference/index
identity exactly as before.

bestPractices: Keep tables readable: enable `striped` and/or `hoverable` for large datasets, but avoid heavy styling that competes with content.

Prefer smart height over tiny scrollbars: enable `constrainHeight` and set `minConstrainedRows` (default 4). If the viewport would show fewer rows, the table expands; when the dataset exceeds `maxExpandedRows` (default 30), it paginates instead of showing a tiny scroller.
Provide an initial sort (`initialSort`), align numeric columns to the right, and format dates consistently.

Enable selection only when followâ€‘up actions operate on selected rows; use the unified `selectionMode` prop (`'none' | 'single' | 'multiple'`) to match the action design. The preâ€‘0.35 `selectable` prop (`'single' | 'multi'`) is a deprecated alias that still works but devâ€‘warns once and is removed at 1.0 â€” migrate `selectable='multi'` to `selectionMode='multiple'`.

When the table is selectable and the data is refreshed immutably (new row objects fetched or reâ€‘mapped), set `getItemKey` (a property name like `getItemKey="id"` or a function `getItemKey={(row) => row.id}`) so selection is tracked by a stable key and survives the refresh. Without it, selection falls back to object identity and is cleared whenever the row objects are replaced. The preâ€‘0.35 `rowKey` prop is a deprecated alias of `getItemKey` (same shape; removed at 1.0).
Keep column count reasonable on narrow screens; prefer fewer, more meaningful columns over many cramped ones. Use succinct headers.
Expose `onRowClick` when rows drive navigation or drillâ€‘ins so the parent surface can react without wiring bespoke click handlers into each cell.

examples.title: Basic striped table
Singleâ€‘select with initial sort
Selection that survives a data refresh (getItemKey)

Auto pagination via minConstrainedRows
Force pagination with page size
Row click actions
